# .grove/ ‚Äî trellis's grove dials

*Grove-managed dial-explainer (regenerated by `/grove:refresh`). Explains the
**effect** of each consumer-facing dial; the normative operating model itself is
never restated here (grove adr-0008).*

trellis is a **grove consumer** (grove adr-0026, the thin-vendor boundary): the
agent roles are **plugin-carried** ‚Äî the thirteen roles load automatically as
`grove:<role>` subagents from the installed grove plugin (pinned in this repo's
`CLAUDE.md` managed block, `grove plugin@<version>`), and are **never vendored**
into `.claude/agents/`. This directory holds only what the consumer owns.

Effect of each dial:

- **`gates.toml`** ‚Äî yours: who must approve at each of the four gates
  (`intent` / `spec` / `build` / `ship`), `human` or `agent`. The floor
  (grove adr-0018): at least one human-owned intent-locus gate ‚Äî `intent =
  "human"` OR `ship = "human"` ‚Äî validated on every run-sequencing read. Switch
  presets with `/grove:set-profile`, or hand-edit a single row. Seeded from the
  `steward` preset.
- **`config.toml`** ‚Äî yours: the shared role tokens (adr-0026 D3) grove agents
  resolve at use time ‚Äî test / typecheck / lint commands, corpus paths, rubric
  paths. Values are **verified priors**: an agent verifies before relying,
  discloses a mismatch loudly, and routes the fix back here rather than silently
  working around a stale value. "None exists yet" is an honest value.
- **`agents/<role>.md`** ‚Äî yours, optional: per-role addenda (local rules,
  worked examples) a generic `grove:<role>` agent reads when present and
  replaces with judgment when absent. Currently one: `run-resumer.md` (trellis's
  branch-naming convention).
- **`internal/`** ‚Äî grove's: the gate-profile floor-guard machinery
  (`internal/gates/`) and the C1 enforcement defaults
  (`internal/enforcement.toml`), re-copied verbatim by `/grove:refresh`; **not a
  consumer surface.** `gates.toml` is resolved through
  `internal/gates/bin/resolve-profile.mjs`.

## trellis's own `corpus-reviewer` coexists with the plugin's (adr-0026 D5)

The `corpus-reviewer` lineage originated in trellis (grove adr-0001). Trellis's
`.claude/agents/corpus-reviewer.md` is a **repo-owned role**, not a vendored
generic: it carries trellis-specific inline rule content in its family checks
and trellis-only repo-typed checks (signature-catalog coverage, profile‚Üícatalog
resolution, evidence and intent-gate floors; `spec-0002` ¬ß4) the generic role
does not. Namespacing makes coexistence collision-free by construction ‚Äî
trellis's `corpus-reviewer` and the plugin's `grove:corpus-reviewer` are
distinct names. `/grove:setup` neither adds nor removes anything under
`.claude/agents/`.

## The operating model is plugin-carried, not restated here (adr-0026 D7)

The lifecycle enum, the versioning grammar, and the artifact edge taxonomy ship
in the grove plugin ‚Äî per the grove lifecycle / versioning / relations
companions, `plugin@<stamp>` (the stamp in this repo's `CLAUDE.md`). No copy of
the companions is installed under `.grove/` (adr-0026 D7 retired the old
`.grove/internal/{lifecycle,versioning,relations}.md`).
